Lycopodioides geniculata, a member of the Lycopodiaceae family, is a type of club moss, a small, low-growing, and non-vascular plant. The health benefits of club mosses are largely unverified, but they may possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Lycopodioides species, such as Lycopodium alkaloids (e.g., lycopodine, selaginelladiene), may contribute to these potential health benefits. In traditional medicine, club mosses are often used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
